title: jacobaea vulgaris subsp. gotlandica
intro: Jacobaea vulgaris subsp. gotlandica, also known as Gotland ragwort, is a subspecies of the ragwort plant, a flowering perennial native to the island of Gotland in Sweden. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Jacobaea vulgaris subsp. gotlandica include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing digestive problems, making it a popular herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of Gotland ragwort include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make topical creams, salves, and ointments for skin conditions and wounds.
